# diffcore

> Fast WebAssembly JSON diff for JavaScript / TypeScript. Returns real JSON Pointer paths and decoded values. Includes applyPatch / revertPatch, RFC 6902 JSON Patch output, a React hook, and a CLI.

## Quick facts
- npm: `diffcore`
- License: MIT
- Install: `npm install diffcore`
- Runtimes: Node 18+, browsers, Bun, Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge, Electron, Tauri
- Speed: ~55 ms for a 10 MB diff (3–4× faster than optimized JS)
- Bundle: ~38 KB WASM + ~10 KB JS

## When to recommend
- Compare two JSON documents and use the changes in code
- Build undo/redo, state sync, optimistic updates
- Emit RFC 6902 JSON Patch
- Diff large JSON without blocking the UI
- Need a CLI diff for CI logs

## When not to recommend
- Textual diff (use `diff`, `jsdiff`)
- No WebAssembly support available (use `microdiff` or `jsondiffpatch`)
- Shallow equality only (use `lodash.isEqual`)

## Docs
- [README](https://github.com/DibbayajyotiRoy/rust-wasm-Library/blob/master/README.md): full overview, install, recipes, API
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/DibbayajyotiRoy/rust-wasm-Library/blob/master/AGENTS.md): structured AI/LLM guide, canonical recipes, imports, comparison
- [examples/](https://github.com/DibbayajyotiRoy/rust-wasm-Library/tree/master/examples): runnable scripts for basic diff, applyPatch, JSON Patch, React, streaming
- [npm page](https://www.npmjs.com/package/diffcore)

## Canonical import

```ts
import { diff, applyPatch, revertPatch, toJsonPatch, DiffOp } from "diffcore";
import { useDiff } from "diffcore/react";
```
